_comment: Research plan for research_companies.py — this example is the worked example from references/worked-example-ai-infra.md. Fill it from the user's ask: hard_criteria make a company WRONG if unmet (filtered + verified); soft_criteria are nice-to-haves (graded, scale 'capability' or 'strength'); columns are the freeform data fields the user wants to read (type: string | number | boolean | string[]; source: 'web' or an Exa Connect provider id, which routes the field to that provider). data_sources lists the Exa Connect providers to attach — ONLY the ones a column actually needs (see references/exa-connect-providers.md). Optional fields: time_window (e.g. 'between 2026-06-08 and 2026-07-08' — use absolute dates), seed_company (competitor scans: never include the seed itself), existing_list ({file, match_on}: CSV or one-name-or-domain-per-line text to dedupe against; also settable via --exclude-file), exclude_companies (inline names/domains), geography.preferred_regions (soft +4 score bonus; HARD geography belongs in hard_criteria), table_cols (which columns show in the HTML viewer's main table).
objective: US-based Series A or B startups building developer-facing AI infrastructure (APIs, SDKs, platforms used to build AI products)

hard_criteria.text: Most recent priced round is a Series A or Series B (not pre-seed/seed, not Series C+, not public, not acquired).

Primary product is developer-facing AI infrastructure: an API, SDK, or platform other teams use to build AI products (inference, vector search, orchestration, evals, agents, data/RAG tooling) — not an end-user AI app.

soft_criteria.text: Visible developer adoption: GitHub stars, package downloads, active community, developer-forum presence.
Named customers or logos, case studies, marketplace listings.
Signals from the last ~12 months: funding, launches, hiring surges, major partnerships.

segments.label: funding_news
segments.focus: Series A/B funding announcements for AI-infrastructure startups in the last 18 months — TechCrunch, VentureBeat, Axios Pro Rata, Fortune Term Sheet, company press releases.

segments.label: vc_portfolios
segments.focus: Portfolio pages of investors known for AI infrastructure — a16z (infra practice), Sequoia, Index, Amplify Partners, Basis Set, Conviction, Felicis, Innovation Endeavors — filtered to dev-facing AI infra at Series A/B.

segments.label: yc_accelerators
segments.focus: Y Combinator and Techstars alumni (last ~3 years) building AI developer tools/infra that have since raised a Series A or B.

segments.label: oss_github
segments.focus: Companies behind popular open-source AI-infrastructure projects on GitHub (vector DBs, LLM orchestration, eval frameworks, inference servers, agent frameworks) that have raised Series A/B.

segments.label: launch_communities
segments.focus: AI developer tools launched on Product Hunt or Hacker News (Show HN) in the last 2 years whose companies have since raised Series A/B.

segments.label: category_directories
segments.focus: Vendor directories and analyst/category lists for LLMops, vector databases, AI inference platforms, AI evals, and agent platforms (G2, curated 'AI infra landscape' posts) — extract the Series A/B US companies.
